Title: ROS-stimulated protein lysine acetylation is required for crown root development in rice

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Root-specific expression pattern inferred for ROS homeostasis-related proteins/peroxidases in rice roots
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Crown roots/root meristem; transcript and protein levels decreased in wox11 roots
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Balances cell division and differentiation in root meristem; required for crown root development
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes ROS/H2O2 accumulation and crown root development; loss of function reduces ROS levels and crown root number
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PRX111 is a direct WOX11 target whose transcript is reduced in wox11 roots. 2. CRISPR knockout of PRX111 lowers root ROS/H2O2 accumulation. 3. prx111 mutants produce fewer crown roots, phenocopying wox11 in part.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; EMSA; dual-luciferase assay; ChIP-qPCR; CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; DAB staining; crown root phenotyping
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: KI treatment lowers H2O2; exogenous H2O2 restores ROS-related defects in wox11 context
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Ubiquitin promoter (Cas9, constitutive); U3/6 promoter (sgRNA); 35S promoter (WOX11 effector, constitutive)
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Proteomics; acetylproteomics; prior transcriptomics; supplementary data linked to article DOI
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to improve crown root system architecture by maintaining optimal ROS homeostasis, but careful dosage control is needed because ROS balance is developmental-context dependent
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Altered ROS homeostasis may affect oxidative stress responses and other developmental processes
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although PRX111 is an enzyme, in this paper it is primarily used as a ROS-homeostasis/output gene linking WOX11 to root phenotype rather than being analyzed for nutrient metabolite biosynthesis. The study further connects ROS to lysine acetylation of proteins enriched in nitrogen metabolism and protein synthesis.

Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Functions in crown root meristem/root tissues; studied in crown roots
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Required signal molecule for crown root development; promotes protein lysine acetylation of proteins enriched in nitrogen metabolism and protein synthesis pathways
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: ROS homeostasis leading to protein lysine acetylation state control
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential activator of root ROS accumulation and upstream controller of ROS-dependent protein acetylation required for crown root development
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates PRX genes, especially PRX111, to promote ROS/H2O2 accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes ROS/H2O2 accumulation, high protein lysine acetylation, and crown root development; mutation causes low ROS, hypo-acetylation, and fewer crown roots
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. WOX11 is required to maintain ROS homeostasis in crown root meristem cells. 2. WOX11 directly binds and activates PRX genes, especially PRX111. 3. Reduced ROS in wox11 elevates HDAC activity, lowers protein lysine acetylation, and impairs crown root development; exogenous H2O2 partially rescues these defects.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: wox11 mutant phenotyping; proteomics; H2O2/ROS assays; EMSA; dual-luciferase assay; ChIP-qPCR; acetylproteomics; KI and H2O2 treatments; HDAC activity assay
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: KI decreases H2O2 and mimics wox11; exogenous H2O2 restores ROS and protein acetylation in wox11
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter (WOX11 effector, constitutive); ubiquitin promoter (transgene/Cas9 contexts, constitutive)
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Proteomics; quantitative acetylproteomics; transcriptomic analysis referenced in study; supplementary datasets via article DOI
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate regulator for engineering stronger crown root systems through controlled ROS signaling, but manipulation should avoid excessive ROS perturbation and unintended developmental effects
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Misregulation may disturb ROS balance, acetylation homeostasis, hormone-regulated development, and stress responses
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The focal paper centers on root development rather than a classic nutrient accumulation trait; however, ROS-dependent acetylation targets are enriched in nitrogen metabolism and peptide/protein biosynthesis pathways, linking WOX11 to metabolism-relevant processes.

Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Analyzed in crown roots via FLAG-tag overexpression lines
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Protein lysine acetylation state affecting nitrogen metabolism-related proteins
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Modulates activities of proteins enriched in organonitrogen metabolism and protein synthesis, thereby influencing crown root development
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent deacetylase contributing to ROS-sensitive hypo-acetylation in wox11 roots
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Multiple acetylated proteins; specific direct targets not fully resolved in this paper
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Deacetylation; activity inhibited by oxidation/redox changes and by sirtinol
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Promotes lower Kac on many proteins when active
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher effective deacetylase activity under low ROS contributes to hypo-acetylation in wox11 roots; inhibition increases protein acetylation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SRT1 redox state changes with KI and H2O2 treatments. 2. H2O2 promotes oxidation consistent with reduced HDAC activity. 3. Sirtinol-sensitive sirtuin activity contributes to the hypo-acetylation profile observed in low-ROS roots.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: FLAG-tag redox shift assay with AMS; sirtinol treatment; quantitative acetylproteomics
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: KI promotes reduced state; H2O2 promotes oxidized state; sirtinol inhibits sirtuin activity
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for tuning protein acetylation-linked root metabolism, though broad pleiotropic effects are likely
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Global acetylation changes may broadly alter development and cellular metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because the Results section experimentally tests its redox sensitivity and contribution to Kac regulation, but it is not the main upstream regulator of ROS.

Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Analyzed in crown roots via FLAG-tag overexpression lines
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Protein lysine acetylation state affecting nitrogen metabolism-related proteins
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Modulates acetylation of metabolism-related proteins associated with crown root growth
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent deacetylase contributing to ROS-sensitive hypo-acetylation in low-ROS roots
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Multiple acetylated proteins; specific direct targets not fully resolved in this paper
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Deacetylation; activity inhibited by oxidation/redox changes and by sirtinol
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Promotes lower Kac on many proteins when active
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Redox-sensitive sirtuin contributing to the decrease in protein acetylation under low ROS
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SRT2 redox status shifts under KI and H2O2 treatments. 2. Oxidation under H2O2 is consistent with lower deacetylase activity. 3. Sirtinol-sensitive sirtuins explain part of the hypo-acetylation pattern in wox11 and KI-treated roots.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: FLAG-tag redox shift assay with AMS; sirtinol treatment; quantitative acetylproteomics
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: KI promotes reduced state; H2O2 promotes oxidized state; sirtinol inhibits sirtuin activity
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential handle for modulating root protein acetylation, with caution due to broad metabolic effects
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Pleiotropic alterations in acetylation and growth
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Experimentally implicated together with SRT1 as part of redox-controlled HDAC activity.

Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Analyzed in crown roots via FLAG-tag overexpression lines
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Controls acetylation of proteins associated with growth-related metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Redox-sensitive HDAC candidate whose oxidation may reduce deacetylation in high-ROS roots
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Candidate contributor to ROS-modulated acetylation changes in crown roots
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HDA710 redox status becomes more reduced with KI and more oxidized with H2O2. 2. This behavior supports redox modulation of HDAC activity in roots. 3. It is proposed as one contributor to low-ROS-induced hypo-acetylation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: FLAG-tag redox shift assay with AMS
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Possible target to alter acetylation-dependent root growth, but evidence here is supportive rather than definitive
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Broad effects on acetylation and gene/protein regulation
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Tested as a candidate HDAC; direct contribution to the crown root phenotype was not genetically validated in this paper.

Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Analyzed in crown roots via FLAG-tag overexpression lines
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Protein lysine acetylation state of non-histone proteins
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Likely modulates acetylation of ribosomal/protein synthesis-related proteins linked to crown root growth
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Candidate non-histone deacetylase responding to ROS to control acetylation of protein synthesis machinery
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Non-histone proteins such as ribosomal proteins (not directly mapped in this paper)
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Deacetylation; likely attenuated by oxidation to a lesser extent
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely contributes to low-ROS-induced hypo-acetylation of non-histone proteins in roots
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HDA714 shows redox-state changes under KI and H2O2 treatments, though weaker than other HDACs. 2. Prior knowledge and pathway enrichment suggest it may target non-histone proteins involved in protein synthesis. 3. It is proposed as a bona fide HDAC responding to WOX11-controlled ROS levels.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: FLAG-tag redox shift assay with AMS
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to modulate translational/protein synthesis acetylation networks in roots, with substantial pleiotropy risk
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Global non-histone acetylation changes may affect growth broadly
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper discusses HDA714 as a plausible mediator based on prior evidence and current enrichment patterns, but direct genetic proof in this study is lacking.
